DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC CAPITAL CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION OF COMPLAINT:
APPEAR: Frank J. Janecek, Esq. 619/231-1058 for pltf & opposing party; Andrew D. Lanphere, Esq. 391-0600 for deft & moving party.
RULING: The Court announced its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with leave to amend to set forth elements of fraud pursuant to applicable civil code sections which are predicate statutes to the UCL claim.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling. (302/AJR/CL) Court Reporter Patricia M. Evezich, CSR #1193

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT GENERAL ELECTRIC CAPITAL CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE:
APPEAR: Frank J. Janecek, Esq. 619/231-1058 for pltf & opposing party; Andrew D. Lanphere, Esq. 391-0600 for deft & moving party.
RULING: The Court announced its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted as to jury demand, denied as to disgorgement.
Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling except the motion to strike jury demand is without prejudice. (302/AJR/CL) Court Reporter Patricia M. Evezich, CSR #1193
